retry.notes: Draw crisp WHITE lines only — do NOT add dark outlines, rims, or drop shadows beside the white lines; each line sits directly on the flat fill colors with no darkening around it.

night.review: The shipped 2026-07 night raw re-inked dark rims beside its white lines (audit:halo rawScore 21.9, shipped haloScore 6.5 — #2 in the catalog); the punch can't remove ink the chalk never drew, so dark mode shows grainy static around every line. After a regen, re-run gen:coloring-fills:audit:halo and expect this page near the catalog floor (≤1), then re-punch (ISSUES Tier 1).
night.why: 2026-07-13 dark-mode composite review — the residual-halo class the halo auditor ranks but nothing reviews; user-visible at normal zoom on this page.

retry.notes: The trash can's body is plain slatted metal — do NOT paint a leaf, recycling emblem, or any logo on the can. Keep the leaves on the ground where the pen draws them. The garbage truck is GREEN all over — cab and body both a deep moonlit green, never navy or blue — and the trash can stays plain grey metal.

night.review: Zoom the can's slats and confirm bare metal — the 2026-07 wave's fill invented a translucent green leaf emblem there (first confirmed fill-side subject-interior invention; no gate or audit scans subject interiors), fixed by a seeded-notes night regen 2026-07-13. Also confirm the cab stays GREEN: without the palette pin a retake painted it sky-navy (the night-contrast trap). Also note: this page's light fill is on the flat-eye list (cab face, light FAIL) — consult the ISSUES flat-eye item before a light-side regen.
night.why: 2026-07-13 ISSUES Tier 1 fix (night regen, this session): the no-emblem notes removed the leaf on every take, but take 1 re-rolled the cab navy-on-navy — the truck-green + grey-can pin was appended and the combined notes shipped clean on take 2 (drift 0.0000, bgLuma 17, lineW 255).

chalk.review: The pen is a 2026-07-13 fresh drawing with RINGED pupils (fresh-outline-regen.md), so the eye-polarity gate is live again for this page. The 3.1 wave's erase-and-redraw --notes + -t 0.4 (gemini-3.1-migration.md) applied only to the OLD solid-pupil pen — do not re-apply it to the current pen.
chalk.why: The wave's chalk whitened the pupils with the sclera (gate-blind on the old solid-pupil pen — no eye cores, vacuous pass); the pen replacement removed that class here. Recorded so the migration table's note is not blindly reused.

retry.notes: Erase the solid white inside each pupil and redraw it: white sclera, a black outlined pupil, and a small white catchlight inside the pupil. Never leave a pupil as one solid white disc.

chalk.review: Solid-pen-eye page: the pen's pupils are solid ink, so findEyeCores finds nothing and the eye-polarity gate passes VACUOUSLY (the gate-blind class in ISSUES). Render the night composite and confirm the pupils stayed black before trusting any chalk of this page.
chalk.why: Sibling police-tall's 3.1-wave chalk whitened both pupils exactly this way and only composite review caught it; an erase-and-redraw note + -t 0.4 fixed it (gemini-3.1-migration.md). Retry text reconstructed. 3.1 resists erase-style edits on solid ink — budget extra attempts.
